title: corydalis longiflora
intro: Corydalis longiflora, a type of flowering plant native to the Himalayan region, belongs to the Papaveraceae family and is commonly known as Himalayan poppy or long-flowered corydalis. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its analgesic, anti-inflammatory, and antispasmodic properties, which are attributed to its alkaloid content, particularly yunnanensine and protopine. The health benefits of corydalis longiflora include pain relief, improved sleep quality, and reduced anxiety and stress levels, making it a popular herbal remedy for conditions such as insomnia and menstrual cramps. Corydalis longiflora is often prepared in various herbal forms, including capsules, tablets, and teas, which can be consumed under the guidance of a healthcare professional.
